titleStmt.title: Telegram from Prison Warden, Frongoch, to the Chief Constable, Dublin, 4 August 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Prison Warden

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: A telegram from the Prison Warden at Frongoch Internment Camp to the Chief Constable, Dublin. The Prison Warden advises that an escaped Irish prisoner, Daniel Davitt, has been re-arrested. Enclosed is a second telegram addressed to apm (Andrew Philip Magill (1871–1941), Private Secretary to Henry Edward Duke (1855–1939), Chief Secretary for Ireland) is a warning that careful watch should be kept and that the escapee may have assumed disguise.Frongoch Internment Camp in Wales was a makeshift prison used for the internment of approximately 1,800 Irishmen in the wake of the Easter Rising, 1916.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers, CSO RP/1916/5620/13549

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Office of Origin and Service Instructions.
O. H. M. S.
Bala To Chief Constable, Dublin Reference my wire 6. 365 of 2nd unit.
Daniel Davitt escaped Irish prisoner
has now been arrested FROM Priswardan Frongoch
Office of Origin and Service Instructions
apms Chester To apm (3) DUBLIN be warned please and careful
watch kept as may have
assumed disguise since escaping addressed
apm Dublin repeated as from Kinmelpark
Manchester Liverpool Rto Holyhead Westmorland
Office of Origin and Service Instructions.
O. H. M. S.
Bala To Chief Constable, Dublin Reference my wire 6. 365 of 2nd inst Daniel
Davitt escaped Irish prisoner has now been
arrested FROM Priswardan Frongoch
